pattern: Counting distinct values and handling k=0 separately reduces the problem to checking, for each unique value, whether its partner exists.
hints: Pairs are counted by unique value, so collapse duplicates with a frequency map first.
When k is zero a pair needs a value appearing at least twice; when k is positive a value needs its value-plus-k partner present.
Build counts of each number; for k>0 count distinct keys v whose v+k also appears, and for k=0 count keys appearing two or more times.

edge_inputs.description: k zero counts repeated values once
k zero but all distinct yields none

stress.description: random values over a narrow range to force duplicates and pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randrange(-30, 31) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, 6)
    return [arr, k]
